formal_full_experiment_rule: Fresh-run contracts are ready; black-box upgrade claims require provider execution plus project-specific postrun promotion gates.

projects.audit_v5_delta: unchanged_from_v4; reviewer manifest v6 now indexes fresh-run hardening for black-box projects
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Scoped white-box claim is locally best-paper-ready: miss concentration, paired component deltas, negative controls, and no-retry boundaries are locked.
forbidden_claims: provider-general claim outside admitted white-box cells
projects.main_claim_allowed: structured provenance watermark over admitted white-box model cells

overfit_risk.reason: Family/scale breadth, negative controls, ablations, and miss taxonomy reduce overfit risk; residual risk is paper overclaiming.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Keep no-retry and natural-generation boundaries explicit.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0/48,000 negative hits plus 8 attack conditions provide strong coverage.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Baseline/control role boundaries are explicit and no support-only cell is promoted.

claim_boundary.rationale: No-retry and provider-general claims are explicitly forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: 72,000 white-box records, 10 admitted models, 5 families, all scale buckets, and 43,200 ablation rows.

innovation.rationale: Structured AST/CFG/SSA provenance with keyed schedule and ECC is a real mechanism.

method_rigor.rationale: Fail-closed detector, clean controls, generation-changing ablation, and row-level miss attribution now close the main rigor gap.

reproducibility.rationale: Hash-bound manifests and scripts exist; full rerun remains GPU/model dependent.

statistics.rationale: CI, denominators, paired component deltas, and mandatory miss disclosure are now locked in reviewer-facing artifacts.

projects.audit_v4_delta: Closed the reviewer-facing row-traceability P2; effect and fresh-v3 evidence remain P1.
projects.audit_v5_delta: Fresh-run contract and provider-readiness v2 close launch-schema risk; effect/fresh-output P1 remains.
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: The current DeepSeek evidence supports only a conservative sparse null-audit; positive-control sensitivity and fresh v3 postrun evidence remain insufficient for a best-paper-level detection claim.
forbidden_claims: evidence that non-signals prove absence of contamination
v3 live claim before the postrun promotion gate passes
projects.main_claim_allowed: DeepSeek-only curator-side sparse null-audit with hash-bound transcript retention

overfit_risk.reason: Effect is weak and must not be optimized through threshold tuning; v3 must be preregistered and hash-complete.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Keep sparse-audit claim and report positive-control misses.

remaining_p1: Effect is still too weak for any detection/prevalence claim: final boundary is 6/300 and positive-control sensitivity is 170/300.
A fresh frozen v3 DeepSeek run with full prompt/raw/structured/task/record hashes is still required before any claim upgrade; the execution contract is ready, provider execution is not.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0/300 negative controls and row hashes are strong, but fresh v3 controls remain needed for an upgrade.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Controls and official/control boundaries are clean; support rows remain excluded.

claim_boundary.rationale: High-recall, prevalence, provider-accusation, and v3-live claims remain forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: 300 live rows plus controls are adequate for sparse null-audit, not for a best-paper-level detection claim.

innovation.rationale: Curator-side sparse null-audit remains a coherent, scoped protocol.

method_rigor.rationale: Final claim lock, positive-miss taxonomy, support exclusion, and legacy row traceability now make the low-effect boundary explicit.

reproducibility.rationale: Fresh v3 execution contract, canonical output path, and postrun gate are now machine-bound; provider access remains a runtime blocker, not a schema blocker.

statistics.rationale: CI, 4/300 vs 6/300 boundary, and signal/source discrepancy disclosure are locked.

projects.audit_v4_delta: Closed leakage-scan and latency/query P2s for scoped single-owner claim.
projects.audit_v5_delta: Multi-owner launch contract now closes schema/naming risk; fresh score-vector evidence P1 remains.
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Single-active-owner attribution and source-bound transfer evidence are strong, but perfect single-owner results remain shortcut/leakage-vulnerable until fresh multi-owner score-vector evidence passes.
forbidden_claims: multi-owner attribution from input package alone
shortcut-free or leakage-free claim without fresh owner/task-heldout score vectors
perfect-score language without the anti-leakage caveat

overfit_risk.reason: Single-owner leakage checks reduce shortcut concerns, but broad attribution still needs multi-owner score vectors.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Keep single-active-owner scope until multi-owner postrun passes.

remaining_p1: Broad multi-owner attribution remains non-claim-bearing until fresh 6,000-row DeepSeek score vectors, margin AUC, and owner/task-heldout postrun gates pass; the input/output contract is ready, provider execution is not.

attack_negative_control.rationale: 0 control false attributions and 0 owner-id emissions support the scoped single-owner claim.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Wrong/null/random/utility/same-provider controls are visible and fail closed for multi-owner.

claim_boundary.rationale: Multi-owner and provider-general claims remain forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: APIS-300, 1,200 controls, transfer-900, and 6,000-row multi-owner input are substantial.

innovation.rationale: Active-owner attribution with semantic witnesses, controls, and transfer binding is strong.

method_rigor.rationale: Single-owner leakage/control-emission checks now pass; multi-owner score vectors remain missing.

reproducibility.rationale: The 6,000-row multi-owner input package, canonical score-vector output path, and postrun gate are now bound by a fresh-run contract.

statistics.rationale: Task-cluster transfer CI and control bounds are explicit; rank/AUC still awaits multi-owner outputs.

projects.audit_v4_delta: Closed abstention taxonomy and expert-wording P2s; low coverage/v5 evidence remain P1.
projects.audit_v5_delta: v5 launch/output contract closes final-evidence naming risk; low coverage/fresh-v5 evidence P1 remains.
projects.bestpaper_ready_reason: Current v3 DeepSeek evidence supports selective marker-hidden triage; decisive coverage is only 81/960, so v5 must improve coverage-risk frontier before any stronger audit-classifier claim.
forbidden_claims: expert-signed gold labels or named/institutional expert certification
v5 coverage upgrade before final v5 postrun promotion passes

overfit_risk.reason: Forcing needs-review rows would inflate performance; the row taxonomy preserves abstention honestly.
overfit_risk.required_guard: Improve coverage only through preregistered v5 conjunction, not relabeling.

remaining_p1: Decisive coverage is still only 81/960, so current evidence supports selective triage rather than a strong audit classifier.
Fresh v5 final evidence, coverage-risk frontier, threshold sensitivity, and visible-marker boundary are still missing; the v5 input/output contract is ready, provider execution is not.

attack_negative_control.rationale: Unsafe-pass bound remains 0/960; v5 final rows are still absent.

baseline_control_strength.rationale: Marker-hidden/visible boundary and role-based support wording are explicit.

claim_boundary.rationale: Certificate, harmlessness, automatic-classifier, and signed-expert-label claims remain forbidden.

evidence_workload.rationale: 320 cases and 960 marker-hidden rows are solid.

innovation.rationale: Watermark-as-security-object selective audit remains useful and honestly scoped.

method_rigor.rationale: Abstention is now row-taxonomized, but decisive coverage is still low.

reproducibility.rationale: The final v5 evidence input, runner receipt, frontier, visible-marker boundary, threshold outputs, and postrun gate are now machine-bound.

statistics.rationale: Coverage-risk and abstention burden are now first-class, with row-level taxonomy.
